title: podman machine start fail on MacOS Monterey Apple M1 Pro
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug


**Description**

<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. start machine
```yaml
MacBook-Pro:~ jianzhang$ podman machine init
Error: podman-machine-default: VM already exists

MacBook-Pro:~ jianzhang$ podman machine start
Starting machine "podman-machine-default"
Waiting for VM ...
Error: qemu exited unexpectedly with exit code -1, stderr: qemu-system-x86_64: Unknown Error

MacBook-Pro:~ jianzhang$ podman pull quay.io/openshift-optional-operators/aosqe-index:v4.11 --authfile ~/.docker/config.json 
Cannot connect to Podman. Please verify your connection to the Linux system using `podman system connection list`, or try `podman machine init` and `podman machine start` to manage a new Linux VM
Error: unable to connect to Podman. failed to create sshClient: connection to bastion host (ssh://core@localhost:50215/run/user/501/podman/podman.sock) failed: dial tcp [::1]:50215: connect: connection refused

```

**Describe the results you received:**

Error: qemu exited unexpectedly with exit code -1, stderr: qemu-system-x86_64: Unknown Error

**Describe the results you expected:**

Podman start machine successfully

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**
MacOS version
```yaml
MacBook-Pro:~ jianzhang$ uname -a
Darwin MacBook-Pro 21.6.0 Darwin Kernel Version 21.6.0: Thu Sep 29 20:13:56 PDT 2022; root:xnu-8020.240.7~1/RELEASE_ARM64_T6000 arm64
MacBook-Pro:~ jianzhang$ sw_vers
ProductName:	macOS
ProductVersion:	12.6.1
BuildVersion:	21G217
```

**Output of `podman version`:**

```console
MacBook-Pro:~ jianzhang$ podman version
Cannot connect to Podman. Please verify your connection to the Linux system using `podman system connection list`, or try `podman machine init` and `podman machine start` to manage a new Linux VM
Error: unable to connect to Podman. failed to create sshClient: connection to bastion host (ssh://core@localhost:50215/run/user/501/podman/podman.sock) failed: dial tcp [::1]:50215: connect: connection refused
```

**Output of `podman info`:**

```console
MacBook-Pro:~ jianzhang$ podman info
Cannot connect to Podman. Please verify your connection to the Linux system using `podman system connection list`, or try `podman machine init` and `podman machine start` to manage a new Linux VM
Error: unable to connect to Podman. failed to create sshClient: connection to bastion host (ssh://core@localhost:50215/run/user/501/podman/podman.sock) failed: dial tcp [::1]:50215: connect: connection refused
```

**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman` or `brew info podman`):**

```console
MacBook-Pro:~ jianzhang$ brew info podman
Warning: Treating podman as a formula.
==> podman: stable 4.2.1 (bottled), HEAD
Tool for managing OCI containers and pods
https://podman.io/
/usr/local/Cellar/podman/4.2.1 (178 files, 48.5MB) *
  Poured from bottle on 2022-11-15 at 20:33:54
From: https://github.com/Homebrew/homebrew-core/blob/HEAD/Formula/podman.rb
License: Apache-2.0
==> Dependencies
Build: go-md2man ✘, go@1.18 ✘
Required: qemu ✔
==> Options
--HEAD
	Install HEAD version
==> Caveats
Bash completion has been installed to:
  /usr/local/etc/bash_completion.d
==> Analytics
install: 34,162 (30 days), 78,019 (90 days), 231,955 (365 days)
install-on-request: 31,811 (30 days), 74,213 (90 days), 227,457 (365 days)
build-error: 27 (30 days)
```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md)**


Yes, checked the  Troubleshooting Guide, but not found the solution.

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**
MacOS Monterey

comments.body: @jianzhangbjz did you by any chance restored homebrew apps from the backup from Intel Mac? Native podman builds for M1 should use `qemu-system-aarch64` binary, not the x86_64 one.
No, just followed this https://podman.io/getting-started/installation, and ran `brew install podman`. Where can I get that `qemu-system-aarch64` binary? Thanks!

I reinstall `homebrew`, but got the below error.
```yaml
MacBook-Pro:~ jianzhang$ sudo /bin/bash -c "$(curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Homebrew/install/master/uninstall.sh)"
Password:
...
MacBook-Pro:~ jianzhang$ /bin/bash -c "$(curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Homebrew/install/HEAD/install.sh)"
==> Checking for `sudo` access (which may request your password)...
Password:
==> This script will install:
/opt/homebrew/bin/brew
/opt/homebrew/share/doc/homebrew
/opt/homebrew/share/man/man1/brew.1
/opt/homebrew/share/zsh/site-functions/_brew
/opt/homebrew/etc/bash_completion.d/brew
/opt/homebrew
...
MacBook-Pro:~ jianzhang$ brew info
0 kegs, 64B

MacBook-Pro:~ jianzhang$ brew install podman
...
MacBook-Pro:~ jianzhang$ podman machine init
Error: podman-machine-default: VM already exists
MacBook-Pro:~ jianzhang$ 
MacBook-Pro:~ jianzhang$ podman machine start
Starting machine "podman-machine-default"
Waiting for VM ...
Error: dial unix /var/folders/2c/4whhm34n7892mf9l2j02cf480000gn/T/podman/podman-machine-default_ready.sock: connect: connection refused
```

```yaml
MacBook-Pro:~ jianzhang$ podman info
Error: failed to connect: dial tcp [::1]:50215: connect: connection refused
```

It works well after removing the old `machine`.
```yaml
MacBook-Pro:~ jianzhang$ podman machine list
NAME                     VM TYPE     CREATED     LAST UP     CPUS        MEMORY      DISK SIZE
podman-machine-default*  qemu        5 days ago  5 days ago  1           2.147GB     107.4GB
MacBook-Pro:~ jianzhang$ podman machine stop
Machine "podman-machine-default" stopped successfully
MacBook-Pro:~ jianzhang$ podman machine list
NAME                     VM TYPE     CREATED     LAST UP     CPUS        MEMORY      DISK SIZE
podman-machine-default*  qemu        5 days ago  5 days ago  1           2.147GB     107.4GB
MacBook-Pro:~ jianzhang$ podman machine rm podman-machine-default

The following files will be deleted:

/Users/jianzhang/.ssh/podman-machine-default
/Users/jianzhang/.ssh/podman-machine-default.pub
/Users/jianzhang/.config/containers/podman/machine/qemu/podman-machine-default.ign
/Users/jianzhang/.local/share/containers/podman/machine/qemu/podman-machine-default_fedora-coreos-37.20221106.2.1-qemu.x86_64.qcow2
/Users/jianzhang/.local/share/containers/podman/machine/podman-machine-default/podman.sock
/Users/jianzhang/.local/share/containers/podman/machine/qemu/podman-machine-default_ovmf_vars.fd
/Users/jianzhang/.config/containers/podman/machine/qemu/podman-machine-default.json


Are you sure you want to continue? [y/N] y

MacBook-Pro:~ jianzhang$ podman machine init
Downloading VM image: fedora-coreos-37.20221106.2.1-qemu.aarch64.qcow2.xz: done  
Extracting compressed file
Image resized.
Machine init complete
To start your machine run:

	podman machine start

MacBook-Pro:~ jianzhang$ podman machine start
Starting machine "podman-machine-default"
Waiting for VM ...
Mounting volume... /Users/jianzhang:/Users/jianzhang

This machine is currently configured in rootless mode. If your containers
require root permissions (e.g. ports < 1024), or if you run into compatibility
issues with non-podman clients, you can switch using the following command: 

	podman machine set --rootful

API forwarding listening on: /Users/jianzhang/.local/share/containers/podman/machine/podman-machine-default/podman.sock

The system helper service is not installed; the default Docker API socket
address can't be used by podman. If you would like to install it run the
following commands:

	sudo /opt/homebrew/Cellar/podman/4.3.1/bin/podman-mac-helper install
	podman machine stop; podman machine start

You can still connect Docker API clients by setting DOCKER_HOST using the
following command in your terminal session:

	export DOCKER_HOST='unix:///Users/jianzhang/.local/share/containers/podman/machine/podman-machine-default/podman.sock'

Machine "podman-machine-default" started successfully
MacBook-Pro:~ jianzhang$ podman info

    apparmorEnabled: false

MacBook-Pro:~ jianzhang$ podman pull quay.io/openshift-qe-optional-operators/ocp4-index:latest
Trying to pull quay.io/openshift-qe-optional-operators/ocp4-index:latest...
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob sha256:cbaae6939760a17f2a7693a3fd874086f92c2c38d2620219b1b3111186a9d722

Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
8af7c73f2b60554b53be11cba1dbb2176ff6ca4418b0d6f62c9458e36ebef6ee
MacBook-Pro:~ jianzhang$ podman list
Error: unrecognized command `podman list`
Try 'podman --help' for more information
MacBook-Pro:~ jianzhang$ podman images
REPOSITORY                                          TAG         IMAGE ID      CREATED      SIZE
quay.io/openshift-qe-optional-operators/ocp4-index  latest      8af7c73f2b60  4 hours ago  388 MB
```
Close it, the solution is:

1. reinstall homebrew
2. remove old machine
3. init and start the machine by following https://podman.io/getting-started/installation
